name: spacefast
description: Publish files and folders to Spacefast from Claude Desktop. Permanent URLs, immutable versions, anonymous publish with a claim link.
long_description: Spacefast is the static host where AI-made things are kept. This extension connects Claude Desktop via the official Spacefast CLI MCP server, giving Claude tools to publish local files, orchestrate signed uploads, import archives, claim anonymous spaces, inspect status and version history, diff versions, roll back, read logs, run approval-resumable workflows, and make bounded workspace edits.

api_token.title: Spacefast API token
api_token.description: Your Spacefast account token (from spacefast.com/account/tokens). Leave blank to publish anonymously.

tools.name: search_docs
tools.description: Search Spacefast's bundled product documentation in-session — guides, CLI reference, platform API reference, and recipes — without leaving the loop or fetching a URL. An exact topic slug (e.g. "publishing", "api/spaces") returns that page's full body; any other query ranks matches. Each result includes the doc body, so this is enough to self-correct. Tier-aware: "full" (default) covers the standard set, "essential" narrows to the core, "reference" widens to advanced docs. Use category "workflow" for agent/MCP workflow guidance

tools.name: feedback
tools.description: Send a bug report, docs issue, limit problem, idea, or other feedback to Spacefast support. Use this when an agent is stuck after surfacing an error code, docsUrl, and requestId

tools.name: publish
tools.description: Publish a local directory, file, or archive (or inline files) to Spacefast and get a live URL in seconds. Without spaceId, On-Device MCP first reuses the space this directory is already linked to (.spacefast/space.json or .spacefast/state.json, walking up from the workspace root) and only creates a brand-new space when no link exists or createNew is true. Anonymous publishes return the claim link, its expiry, and a shown-once claim token: save the token (.spacefast/state.json when you have filesystem access, otherwise conversation memory), never print it, and always give the user the claim URL and expiry. To update the space later, publish with the same spaceId and that token as claimToken (the Authorization bearer). Pass spaceId (from a publish receipt or spaces_list, plus claimToken for unclaimed spaces) to publish a new version of an existing space. Retries are de-duplicated automatically, so a re-run after a network blip will not double-publish. Always paste the returned shareBlurb into your reply

tools.name: prepare_publish
tools.description: Create a manifest-based publish session and return signed upload instructions without sending file bytes through MCP. Use this for large files, cloud agents, or any publish where base64/inline content would be awkward. Pass spaceId (from a publish receipt or spaces_list) to add a version to an existing space. Upload returned paths to the signed upload URLs, then call finalize_publish. For On-Device path-based publishes, prefer the publish tool through On-Device MCP

tools.name: resume_publish
tools.description: Get fresh signed upload instructions for an unfinished manifest-based publish. Use this when an upload token expired, more files are needed, or a large publish is being continued

tools.name: finalize_publish
tools.description: Finalize a manifest/open upload session after all advertised files have been uploaded to the signed upload URLs. This compiles routing headers and optionally promotes the version live. Retries are de-duplicated automatically

tools.name: import_from_url
tools.description: Import a spacefast_export_v1 archive from a public HTTPS URL into an existing claimed space. Use this for cloud-agent big-file handoff when the archive already lives at a URL; no base64 or local filesystem access is needed

tools.name: prepare_import_upload
tools.description: Create an import handle that returns signed upload instructions for one spacefast_export_v1 archive. PUT the archive to `${archiveUpload.baseUrl}/${archiveUpload.files[0]}` with the upload token, then call finalize_import_upload

tools.name: resume_import_upload
tools.description: Fetch an existing upload-sourced archive import and return fresh archiveUpload instructions. Use this when an archive upload token expired before finalize_import_upload

tools.name: finalize_import_upload
tools.description: Finalize an upload-sourced archive import after the archive has been uploaded with prepare_import_upload instructions

tools.name: claim
tools.description: Attach an anonymously published space to the authenticated account or a team, removing its expiry. Requires authenticated Spacefast credentials. Without them, give the user the claim URL and expiry from the publish receipt so they can claim it themselves in the browser; after they claim, call continue_space to exchange your saved claim token for a durable access token and keep publishing

tools.name: claim_status
tools.description: Check the honest state of an anonymous publish: whether the space is still unclaimed (plus the claim URL and expiry) or already claimed, and — once claimed — whether agent continuation is available, used, or unavailable. Authenticates with the claim token (from the publish receipt or .spacefast/state.json). Use it to decide between sharing the claim link, calling continue_space, or asking the user for an access token

tools.name: continue_space
tools.description: Exchange an anonymous claim token, exactly once, for a durable space-scoped access token after the space's owner claimed it with agent continuation enabled. Use this when a publish or status call fails with space_claimed_credential_available. The claim token comes from the input or, On-Device, from .spacefast/state.json; the returned credential.accessToken is shown once — save it (On-Device MCP saves it to .spacefast/state.json automatically) and use it as the Authorization bearer for future publishes to this space. A second exchange fails with continuation_used; if the owner declined continuation or the window expired it fails with continuation_unavailable

tools.name: spaces_list
tools.description: List the Spacefast spaces your account can access, newest first. Use this for cold start when you have an account but no spaceId yet: each entry returns space.id, slug, and liveUrl to pass to publish, status, versions, and the other space tools

tools.name: teams_list
tools.description: List the Spacefast teams your account can act for, newest first. Use this for cold start to discover a team ref (id or slug) to scope spaces_list or target a new publish

tools.name: create_team
tools.description: Create a new Spacefast team owned by your account and get its team ref (id and slug) back. Use this for cold start when you need a team to publish or claim a space into and teams_list shows none you want. Requires authenticated Spacefast credentials tied to a user account; machine/team-scoped credentials cannot own a team. Retries are de-duplicated automatically, so a re-run after a network blip will not create a second team

tools.name: status
tools.description: Get the current state of a space in one call: live URL, claim/expiry state, configuration, and its most recent versions

tools.name: versions
tools.description: List a space's immutable versions, newest first, with status, immutable URL, source, and description for each. Use the returned ids with the rollback and diff tools

tools.name: diff
tools.description: Show what changed between two versions of a space as per-file added/modified/deleted statuses with unified text patches. Compares against the immediately previous version unless baseVersionId is set

tools.name: rollback
tools.description: Make an earlier ready version live again without rebuilding or re-uploading. Promotes the given version to the live channel; use the versions tool to find version ids. Waits for the live pointer to move by default; if promotion is still in progress it returns an operation handle to poll instead of failing. Pass async to queue and return that handle immediately

tools.name: operation_status
tools.description: Poll a long-running Spacefast operation by its operation id and drive it to completion without blocking. Use the operationId from a long-op job handle (rollback in async mode, import_from_url, finalize_import_upload, or any result carrying job.poll.operationId). Returns the operation status (queued, running, succeeded, failed, or canceled), progress, diagnostics, a done flag, and a poll hint until it settles

tools.name: logs
tools.description: Fetch recent access or runtime log entries for a space, within the plan's retention window. Use access logs for traffic questions and runtime logs for serving diagnostics. For build/publish logs of one version, use the build_logs tool instead

tools.name: build_logs
tools.description: Fetch the publish and finalize log entries for one version — the build log for diagnosing why a publish failed or stalled. Use the versions tool to find the versionId, typically the most recent one from a publish receipt

tools.name: builds_list
tools.description: List a space's builds (remote repository or source archive execution runs), newest first, as compact rows: id, status, input kind, and the version it produced or reused. Use build_get for the full immutable input and settings snapshot of one build

tools.name: build_get
tools.description: Fetch one build by its build id (bld_...): current status, immutable input, settings snapshot, target, diagnostics, and the produced or reused version. Use builds_list to find the buildId

tools.name: build_logs_by_id
tools.description: Fetch runner log lines for one build (bld_...) — the source-fetch/install/build-command output for a repository or source archive build run. Use builds_list or build_get to find the buildId. For a version's publish/finalize log, use the build_logs tool instead

tools.name: build_retry
tools.description: Retry a terminal build (bld_...) with its original immutable input and settings, queuing a fresh build run. Only terminal builds (succeeded, failed, canceled, skipped) can be retried; an active build errors. A source-archive build whose bytes never finished uploading returns fresh upload instructions instead of queuing immediately

tools.name: build_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a queued, uploading, or running build (bld_...). Idempotent: canceling an already-terminal build returns its current state unchanged instead of erroring

tools.name: repository_status
tools.description: Get a space's repository connection summary: git host, repository, sync status, last sync error, and auto-deploy flags. Returns a clean not-connected result (connected: false) when the space has no repository connection of the requested type yet, instead of erroring

tools.name: diagnose
tools.description: Aggregate a space's current status, recent versions, build logs, and access logs into one ranked findings list so you can self-correct after a failed or stalled publish or build. Read-only: it calls the same endpoints as status, versions, build_logs, and logs and changes nothing. Each finding has a code, severity, where (location), problem, and a fix hint (plus a docs link when available), most severe first. Pass versionId to focus on one version; otherwise the newest version is diagnosed

tools.name: workspace_list
tools.description: Quickly list files without reading contents by default. On-Device MCP lists the bounded local workspace root; hosted MCP lists the durable virtual workspace for this MCP session

tools.name: workspace_read
tools.description: Read a UTF-8 text file. On-Device MCP reads from the bounded local workspace root; hosted MCP reads from the durable virtual workspace. Refuses absolute paths, path traversal, binary files, large files, and secret-like paths

tools.name: workspace_write
tools.description: Create or replace a workspace file. On-Device MCP writes to the bounded local workspace root; hosted MCP writes to the durable virtual workspace. Use utf8 for text and base64 for small generated binary assets. Returns a diff for text content

tools.name: workspace_apply_patch
tools.description: Apply exact string replacements to workspace files. This is safer for agents than whole-file rewrites when the target text is known. Hosted MCP patches the durable virtual workspace; On-Device MCP patches the bounded local workspace root

tools.name: workspace_diff
tools.description: Show diffs for workspace files changed since this MCP server first read or wrote them. Hosted MCP diffs the durable virtual workspace; On-Device MCP diffs local files under the bounded workspace root

tools.name: execute
tools.description: Run sandboxed JavaScript code against Spacefast tools through a QuickJS code-mode runtime. Use spacefast.search("query"), spacefast.publish({...}), spacefast.workspace.read({...}), tools.search({...}), and tools.describe({name}). Legacy JSON/JSONL workflows are still accepted with mode=workflow or auto detection. Defaults to dryRun=true so mutating calls are planned rather than executed. With dryRun=false, Spacefast can return an approval_required checkpoint for resume

tools.name: resume
tools.description: Resume, approve, or deny a pending execute approval checkpoint. If decision is omitted, Spacefast uses the browser decision recorded for the approval
